message:
When you take your dog for a walk and they stop to sniff EVERYTHING — that lamppost, that patch of grass, that one very specific blade — they're not being stubborn. They're reading the news. 🐶

A dog's sense of smell is almost impossible to wrap your head around. They have somewhere around 300 million scent receptors in their nose, compared to our roughly 6 million, and the part of the brain they devote to analyzing smell is proportionally far larger than ours. That single sniff can tell them who passed by, how long ago, which direction they went, and even something about how that animal was feeling.

So those long sniffing sessions on a walk? That's enrichment — your dog's favorite way to explore the world. Giving them time for a few good "sniffaris" is genuinely good for their brain and mood. 💛

Does your dog have a favorite sniff spot they HAVE to check on every single walk? 🐾
